page.primary_keyword: marbella yacht charter f1 grand prix
page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter for the F1 Grand Prix: Race Weekend at Sea

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix weekend. Watch the race from the sea, anchor near Puerto Banús, and enjoy VIP views from €749. Check availability now.
data.summary: A Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix weekend offers a unique way to watch the race from the sea, with departures from Puerto Banús and VIP amenities on board. Our fleet includes boats from €749 for 2 hours, with all drinks, snacks, and a licensed skipper included.
key_facts: Charters depart from Puerto Banús, just minutes from the F1 paddock.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) to €4,719 for 4 hours (Mangusta 80).
All charters include skipper, fuel, insurance, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA.
Boats accommodate 5–12 guests, ideal for small groups or corporate parties.
Anchor near the circuit for prime race viewing from the water.
Free jet ski included on Mangusta 80 bookings (min. 4 hours).
Flexible timing for qualifying, practice, or race day.
Contact us via WhatsApp for bespoke packages and availability.

faq.q: Can I watch the F1 race directly from a yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, you can anchor your yacht near the Puerto Banús marina area, which offers excellent views of the circuit, especially during the Spanish Grand Prix. The proximity to the paddock means you can see the action from the water while enjoying the comfort of your private charter.

faq.q: What is the best boat for an F1 weekend with a group of friends?
faq.a: For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) is a top choice, with a free jet ski and spacious deck. For smaller parties, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (9–11 pax) offer great value from €749 for 2 hours. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Are drinks and food included in the charter price?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You can also bring your own food or arrange catering via us. The price also covers the skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT.

faq.q: How far in advance should I book for F1 weekend?
faq.a: We recommend booking at least 2–4 weeks ahead, as F1 weekend is peak season and our fleet is limited. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability and secure your preferred boat and time slot.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat for just a few hours on race day?
faq.a: Absolutely. We offer 2-hour and 4-hour slots, as well as full-day charters. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking, while the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 can be booked for 2 hours starting at €749.

faq.q: Is there parking near Puerto Banús for my car?
faq.a: Puerto Banús has several paid parking garages within walking distance of the marina. Spaces fill quickly during F1 weekend, so arrive early or consider a taxi. We can also arrange a driver if needed.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad on race day?
faq.a: The skipper will assess conditions and may suggest a revised route or time. Safety is paramount, and we can reschedule or cancel with a full refund if weather prevents safe sailing. Contact us for our exact policy.

faq.q: Do you provide a skipper who knows the best spots for viewing?
faq.a: Yes, all our skippers are licensed and experienced with the local coastline. They know the ideal anchoring spots near the circuit and can also recommend quieter coves if you want to combine race viewing with a swim.

data.body_html:
The Formula 1 Grand Prix weekend turns Marbella and Puerto Banús into a global party hub. A Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix lets you skip the crowded grandstands and watch the race from the water, with cold drinks, a private deck, and the Mediterranean as your backdrop. Whether you want a quick 2-hour cruise or a full-day VIP experience, our fleet departs directly from Puerto Banús, minutes from the circuit.

Why charter a yacht for the F1 weekend?

Watching the Spanish Grand Prix from a private boat offers a unique perspective. You anchor near the coastline, with clear sightlines to parts of the track, while avoiding the queues and noise of the main circuit. Plus, you have total flexibility: arrive when you want, leave when you want, and enjoy unlimited drinks and snacks included in the price. Our Marbella yacht charter service makes it easy to combine race action with swimming, sunbathing, and exploring the coast.

Our fleet for F1 weekend

We offer a range of boats to suit different group sizes and budgets. All depart from Puerto Banús, the epicentre of F1 activity in Marbella.

BoatLengthCapacityPrice (from)
Astondoa 4012.5m9 pax€749 / 2h
Azimut 3912.5m11 pax€749 / 2h
Mangusta 80 (White/Grey)24m12 pax€4,719 / 4h
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12 paxOn request

For larger groups or corporate hospitality, boats like the Ferretti 94 and Canados 86 offer 12-person capacity and premium amenities. Contact us via WhatsApp for custom pricing and availability.

What’s included in every charter?

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a full drinks package (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) plus light snacks. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the price shown. There are no hidden extras — just show up and enjoy. For the Mangusta 80, a jet ski is included free on 4-hour bookings.

Best boats for race-day viewing

The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is ideal for a group wanting space and a jet ski. Its large flybridge and sun deck offer elevated views. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) is another solid choice, with a spacious interior and outdoor areas. For smaller budgets, the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 provide excellent value at €749 for 2 hours, enough time to cruise near the circuit and enjoy the atmosphere.

How to plan your F1 weekend itinerary

Most clients book a 2–4 hour slot on race day (Sunday) or for qualifying on Saturday. You can also combine a morning cruise to Cabopino or Cala del Faro with an afternoon anchoring near the circuit. Our skippers know the best spots to drop anchor for clear views. If you want a full day, the 8-hour option on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 costs €2,299 and includes time for swimming, lunch, and race viewing.

Puerto Banús: the heart of the action

Puerto Banús is the go-to marina for F1 weekend, with restaurants, bars, and a buzzing atmosphere. All our boats depart from here, so you’re steps from the action. After the race, you can head back to the marina for after-parties or a meal at one of the Golden Mile’s top spots. For a quieter escape, anchor near Marbella Marina or explore the coves around Estepona.

Booking tips and availability

F1 weekend is one of the busiest periods in Marbella. We recommend booking at least 2–4 weeks in advance. Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred boat, date, and time. We’ll confirm availability and send you a booking link. Payment is secure, and cancellations are handled fairly. For the best rates, consider a 2-hour slot on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, or go all-in with the Mangusta 80 for a premium experience.

Combine F1 with coastal exploration

Make the most of your trip by adding a cruise to Cala Cortés or Sotogrande. These spots offer clear water for swimming and a relaxed vibe away from the race crowds. A full-day charter lets you do both: watch the race in the morning, then cruise south for lunch and a swim. Our skippers will tailor the route to your preferences.

Frequently asked questions

Can I watch the F1 race directly from a yacht in Marbella?

Yes, you can anchor your yacht near the Puerto Banús marina area, which offers excellent views of the circuit, especially during the Spanish Grand Prix. The proximity to the paddock means you can see the action from the water while enjoying the comfort of your private charter.

What is the best boat for an F1 weekend with a group of friends?

For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) is a top choice, with a free jet ski and spacious deck. For smaller parties, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (9–11 pax) offer great value from €749 for 2 hours. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Are drinks and food included in the charter price?

Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. You can also bring your own food or arrange catering via us. The price also covers the skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT.

How far in advance should I book for F1 weekend?

We recommend booking at least 2–4 weeks ahead, as F1 weekend is peak season and our fleet is limited. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability and secure your preferred boat and time slot.

Can I charter a boat for just a few hours on race day?

Absolutely. We offer 2-hour and 4-hour slots, as well as full-day charters. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking, while the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 can be booked for 2 hours starting at €749.

Is there parking near Puerto Banús for my car?

Puerto Banús has several paid parking garages within walking distance of the marina. Spaces fill quickly during F1 weekend, so arrive early or consider a taxi. We can also arrange a driver if needed.

What happens if the weather is bad on race day?

The skipper will assess conditions and may suggest a revised route or time. Safety is paramount, and we can reschedule or cancel with a full refund if weather prevents safe sailing. Contact us for our exact policy.

Do you provide a skipper who knows the best spots for viewing?

Yes, all our skippers are licensed and experienced with the local coastline. They know the ideal anchoring spots near the circuit and can also recommend quieter coves if you want to combine race viewing with a swim.

Ready to book your Marbella yacht charter for the F1 Grand Prix? Check our fleet and availability, or message us on WhatsApp to secure your spot. The race weekend is unforgettable — make it even better from the water.
